task_completion.description: Set the observation location to Florence, Italy with the correct coordinates and altitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window or the bottom status bar shows 'Florence', 'Italy', or the coordinates 43.7696° N, 11.2558° E, and altitude 50m.

task_completion.description: Disable atmosphere and landscape, and enable constellation lines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view shows a black background (no atmosphere), no ground/horizon (landscape disabled), and constellation lines are drawn between stars.

task_completion.description: Observe Jupiter and its Galilean moons on January 7, 1610, at 19:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Date/Time window or status bar shows 1610-01-07 and 19:00. The view is centered and zoomed in on Jupiter, with its moons (Io, Europa, Ganymede, Callisto) visible.

task_completion.description: Observe Jupiter and its Galilean moons on January 8, 1610, at 19:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Date/Time window or status bar shows 1610-01-08 and 19:00. The view is centered and zoomed in on Jupiter, with its moons visible.

task_completion.description: Capture a screenshot for each of the two nights.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Screenshot saved' notification appears in Stellarium, or a file manager/image viewer is open showing the saved screenshots in the Pictures/stellarium directory.

task_completion.description: Record the observation dates and a description of the moon position changes in galileo_demo_notes.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file '/home/ga/Desktop/galileo_demo_notes.txt'. The content includes the dates (Jan 7 and Jan 8, 1610) and describes how the positions of the moons changed relative to Jupiter.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent using Stellarium's menus, toolbars, and dialog windows (e.g., Location, Date/Time, Sky and Viewing options) rather than just running terminal commands.

integrity.description: The notes describe actual visual changes observed in the Stellarium view.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in galileo_demo_notes.txt contains a descriptive account of the moons' movements (e.g., shifting from one side of Jupiter to the other), rather than a generic copy-paste of the task description.

integrity.description: The screenshots were genuinely captured from the Stellarium application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots or the process of taking them reflect the actual Stellarium interface configured as requested, without evidence of downloading pre-existing images.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Location: Florence, Italy (43.7696 N, 11.2558 E, 50m). Dates: 1610-01-07 19:00:00 UTC and 1610-01-08 19:00:00 UTC. Target: Jupiter and Galilean moons (Io, Europa, Ganymede, Callisto). Required view settings: Atmosphere OFF, Landscape OFF, Constellation lines ON. Demo notes path: /home/ga/Desktop/galileo_demo_notes.txt. Screenshots directory: /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium.
